# Embodiment Evasion Tax — GPU-node pinned environment (OpenVLA-7B + LIBERO eval layer)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# PROVENANCE (invariant #8 — fetched from source, NOT hand-typed from memory):
#   OpenVLA `pyproject.toml` @ github.com/openvla/openvla (main, fetched 2026-06-03);
#   codec-verified pin = commit c8f03f48 (docs/references/README.md).
#   OpenVLA README "results obtained with": Python 3.10.13, PyTorch 2.2.0,
#   transformers 4.40.1, flash-attn 2.5.5, NVIDIA A100.
#
# Every line is BEST-EFFORT and must be re-checked on Kelvin2 before the env is
# frozen for a logged run: [VERIFY ON THE GPU NODE]. On confirmation, capture the
# real `pip freeze` + CUDA/driver/torch into the run log (playbook §8) and the
# checkpoint hashes into docs/references/README.md.
#
# This file is the OpenVLA + project *pip* layer. Two stacks install separately and are
# NOT pinned here (they bring their own env files — see docs/setup/gpu-runbook.md):
#   - LIBERO sim  : git clone Lifelong-Robot-Learning/LIBERO + libero_requirements.txt
#   - RoboGCG     : its own `robo_env.yml` conda env (likely conflicts with this one
#                   → keep RoboGCG in a SEPARATE env; see runbook §6)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

# --- Python: OpenVLA requires-python is >=3.8; results env = 3.10.13.
#     Keep 3.10 (our local src/evasion_tax is pinned 3.10-compatible for exactly this).

# --- Core deep-learning stack (OpenVLA exact pins) ---
torch==2.2.0                 # [VERIFY] CUDA build must match the Kelvin2 CUDA module (torch 2.2.0 default = cu121)
torchvision==0.17.0
torchaudio==2.2.0
transformers==4.40.1
tokenizers==0.19.1
timm==0.9.10
peft==0.11.1
accelerate>=0.25.0
sentencepiece==0.1.99
draccus==0.8.0
einops
huggingface_hub
json-numpy
jsonlines
protobuf<5                   # <5: TF 2.15 stack uses protobuf 4.x (4.25.x on CSB A5000); only a too-new
                             # tensorflow-metadata needs protobuf>=5.26 (`runtime_version`) — see TF stack note below.
rich

# --- flash-attn: VERIFIED on CSB A5000 (2026-06-18). OpenVLA results env used 2.5.5.
#     Source build (`pip install "flash-attn==2.5.5" --no-build-isolation`) FAILS on this box:
#       it needs nvcc + CUDA_HOME, but CSB has only the driver (595) + torch's bundled cu121
#       runtime — no CUDA toolkit and no sudo. → use the PREBUILT WHEEL (no nvcc):
#       flash_attn-2.5.5+cu122torch2.2cxx11abiFALSE-cp310-cp310-linux_x86_64.whl
#       (official Dao-AILab v2.5.5 release; cu122 wheel runs on cu121 torch + driver 595).
#       cxx11abi must match torch (`python -c "import torch; print(torch._C._GLIBCXX_USE_CXX11_ABI)"`
#       → False on the cu121 pip build → abiFALSE). Confirmed: flash-attn kernel launches, and
#       OpenVLA loads `--attn-impl flash_attention_2` → valid 7-DoF action, peak 14.46 GiB reserved
#       (== sdpa for a single forward; flash-attn's memory win is at GCG batch/seq scale, not here).
#       Do NOT add to a one-shot `pip install -r` — install the wheel separately.
# flash-attn==2.5.5          # prebuilt wheel only on CSB (see above); not a one-shot req line

# --- OpenVLA data dep (git) ---
dlimp @ git+https://github.com/moojink/dlimp_openvla

# --- TensorFlow stack — REQUIRED for the OpenVLA *eval-helper* import chain ---
#     CONFIRMED on CSB A5000 (2026-06-18): the LIBERO eval helpers import as
#       experiments.robot.robot_utils -> prismatic.__init__ (eager) -> ... -> dlimp
#         -> tensorflow_datasets -> tensorflow_metadata
#     so `import experiments.robot.robot_utils` (used by scripts/smoke_libero_episode.py) pulls the
#     whole TF stack AT IMPORT TIME — NOT optional, despite the old "inference-only → likely NOT needed"
#     note (which was wrong for the eval path). TF runs no ops here (the VLA forward is torch/CUDA) → it
#     steals no GPU memory; it is import-only. The startup I/E/W logs (oneDNN, cuDNN/cuFFT/cuBLAS "already
#     registered", TF-TRT) are benign — silence with TF_CPP_MIN_LOG_LEVEL=3 if desired.
#
#     GOTCHA (the one real trap): tfds 4.9.3 puts NO upper bound on tensorflow-metadata, so pip pulled
#     tfmd 1.21.0, whose generated *_pb2.py does `from google.protobuf import runtime_version` (added in
#     protobuf 5.26) → ImportError on the TF-2.15-correct protobuf 4.25.x. FIX = cap tfmd to the TF-2.15 era.
#     Resolved on the box: tensorflow 2.15.0 + tfds 4.9.3 + tensorflow-metadata <1.16 + protobuf 4.25.x.
tensorflow==2.15.0
tensorflow_datasets==4.9.3
tensorflow-metadata<1.16     # <1.16: tfds 4.9.3 caps nothing; tfmd 1.16+ needs protobuf>=5.26 (see GOTCHA)
# tensorflow_graphics==2021.12.3   # NOT pulled by the eval import chain — add only if a script needs it

# --- Embodiment Evasion Tax model-free core (mirrors pyproject.toml; already unit-tested locally) ---
# numpy<2: torch 2.2.0 is built against the numpy-1.x ABI (numpy 2 → "Numpy is not available"
# on torch.from_numpy); OpenVLA's results env also pins numpy 1.x. Verified on CSB A5000 2026-06-17.
numpy>=1.26,<2
scipy>=1.11
scikit-learn>=1.3
pyyaml>=6.0
pydantic>=2.5
matplotlib>=3.8
